raw_input:
[1.0/5] Not worth it!!! Super small and tight!! No I was excited for these, because my brushes always leave sparkles and color all over my make up bags. But these did not work well!! They are so so small and couldn’t even make it up the stick of my brush. It kept getting stuck and was too small. I ended up breaking off the brush part from the stick because of the force I was trying to use to move the silicon up. The small eyeshadow ones were way too small to get the hairs in. It’s almost impossible to get all the hairs into the covering, and not get them sticking to the outside. Also not sure why they have suction cups on the back side of them. Overall it’s a pain to even attempt to put the silicon on the stick and then over the hairs. Not worth it and doesn’t work well. Will probably do more damage to you brush in trying to put it on, than good that’d come from it [3.0/5] Silicone Cover for Brushes - STRONG GRIP The order comes with 9 pieces of the silicone makeup brush covers. There are two (2) different sizes for covering different type of brushes. One type for larger brush for like foundation and the other type of smaller brushes like eye shadow. The cover provides good protection over the brush but also protects the bag that would hold this brush from leaving powder or color marks. The protection part is good but I had mad struggle trying to get the silicone cover on my make up brush. It's not easy and it has an extremely strong grip. Once its grips on something it is so hard to remove or slide down to use the brush. [5.0/5] Keeps makeup organized The silicone brush covers come in different sizes for different types of brushes. You put it over the brush end without bristles and slide it up over the bristles. You can slide it down when you’re using the brush so you don’t have to take it off. There are also covers for applicators for lip gloss. They have a little suction cup on the bottom so you can keep it attached to the container so you don’t lose it. This keeps your lip gloss sanitary because you’re not constantly sticking your finger in the pot. [2.0/5] Nice for a "trinket" present I was disappointed with these silicone covers. The big ones fit the larger brushes I have but continue to slip off when starting brush up on its end. The small ones I couldn't even get on my smaller brushes as the handles were too big. They could be used as a secret Santa gift or a White Elephant exchange but otherwise I wouldn't purchase them again. [5.0/5] cute and must have for makeup needs This silicone make up brush cover is def must have for those who likes make up and don't like messy makeup bag.

Easy to use slip on cover for brush and comes with small ones for smaller ones and even has suction cup to organize better and no more time consuming figuring out which brush is which.

Great use for travel, and easy to store, personal use or gift giving and recommend it. [4.0/5] Good Idea I like these for when I travel. Unfortunately they don't fit all my makeup brushes since some of my brushes are wider than the cover. No big deal. I still use the other ones for the brushes that they fit and are happy with them. [3.0/5] Horrible suction. I have these cute little honey pot lip glosses that came with tiny honey dippers to apply the balm but there is nowhere to store them. So I was excited to see these because I thought I could stick the little covers to the lid and it would be perfect for storing the little dippers. Unfortunately, the little suckers only stay on for a minute or two before they pop off. I have put a drop of water on them which helps the suction cups attach better, but they still fall off after a minute or two. The big brush covers are perfect but the small ones just don't do what they are supposed to, so I don't recommend them. [1.0/5] Awful. Doesn’t work. The little holders for the application tools do NOT stay attached to any container. The suction does not work. [5.0/5] Make up brush cover is great for traveling These little silicone make up brush holders are actually pretty cool they make for a great travel so that if you don’t have time to actually clean off your brushes it won’t get make up all over your bag or clothes. this particular set does not give you a big variety of different sizes is the only downfall. the smaller ones do have a suction cup and they stick to glass perfectly [2.0/5] Good in theory, bad in practice I was excited to find these. I keep my makeup brushes on my bathroom counter and while I am good about cleaning them, I know they are exposed to a lot of dust and germs, so I was hoping that these covers would be a good solution to keep them nice. I was wrong.

Let's start with the three large heads. I was happy to find that these were three different sizes as the main brushes I use have different volumes of bristles. I put them on based on the size of brush and cap. There was no other way to possibly get them onto the brush rather than stick down the heard pull it through until the heart was around the bristles. The silicone was so difficult to slide up my brush handle that I was scared of ripping them and my brush handles are not that thick. Once they were in place I came across my next problem, how to gain access to the brush without fully removing the silicone cover. I somewhat figured out how to use the top hole to flip the cover inside out and expose the bristles, the problem was that in doing so many of the bristles got stuck or were pulled in the wrong direction. I have expensive brushes and I take care of them. I do not want them damaged by a cheap piece of silicone. I tried my best to keep them neat, but after about three days of doing this, the first of the three silicone covers began to rip from this practice. I'm not sure what else I was supposed to do to access my brush. I ended up taking the covers off all three and will maybe use them for traveling, but that's it. They are impractical for day to day use.

No to the small ones. These are just annoying. They are all so tiny and all the same size. I have whole bunch of different sized eye shadow brushes and only one or two and the right sized head to fit in these, which makes them mostly useless for me. I don't get the appeal of the suction cup. The pictures show people sticking them to the bathroom mirror, but once the brush is taken out of the cap and the cap remains on the mirror they are going to be impossible to place back in the cover without taking it off the mirror. Additionally the hole to stick the brushes in the caps is so small that the brush bristles were being bent. Again I'm not going to ruin my brushes that cost several times what the covers cost to keep these annoying caps on them.

The only reason this set is not getting one star from me is that I can use the large ones when traveling to keep my brushes nice in my makeup bag, but otherwise this set is completely useless. [3.0/5] Size Variety These are nice and easy to clean, but the purple ones could use one bigger size, and the smaller ones are all pinky tip size, so there isn't really a medium size here to accommodate eye brushes. There's a suction on the small ones that make it easy to attach to your mirror as you wait to get to it. [5.0/5] perfect for traveling I love these silicone brushholders. I’m a little bit of a Germafobe when it comes to combining all of my make up brushes into one bag, the silicone holders go over the top of your brush head to keep them protected. They’re great for traveling, it comes with three big silicone heads and six little silicone heads for smaller brushes like eyeshadow brushes. Going to be buying more. [4.0/5] Brush protector These are silicone brush covers to keep my wife's makeup brushes clean. They do they job and are especially handy for cleanliness within travel bags. [4.0/5] Great for keeping your brushes clean These are great for travel to keep your brush heads clean before you use them, and the rest of your bag clean after. They have a slit vent at the end for sanitary purposes, and slide on/off easily. The smaller ones work great for your smallest brushes (think eyeliner and concealer), or silicone lip applicators. For that purpose, they also suction to the lid of your lip product, although they don't stay very firmly attached. [5.0/5] Wonderful product This review is for 9 Pcs Silicone Makeup Brush Cover, Reusable Dust proof Makeup Brush Protector.

Wonderful product. They fit well and they keep my brushes from getting dirty. Perfect for traveling. Love the different sizes, love the colors. [5.0/5] They work good I love having those covers to protect my brushes. They work great! [3.0/5] Keeps brushes clean These silicone makeup brush covers are cute and they do help keep your brushes clean. My only issue with these are, the small silicone covers go on tiny eyeshadow applicators that come in eyeshadow palettes. I don't even keep those little eyeshadow applicators. I use small eyeshadow makeup brushes to apply my eyeshadow. It would have been nice to have covers to go over makeup brushes just like the purple heart ones do.
Otherwise it's a cool product. I do like the purple heart covers a lot! [1.0/5] Doesn’t stick! Pretty disappointed with the spoon holders. They only stay suctioned for like 45 minutes. I guess if you want to keep the dust off it still works but I’m pretty bummed

response:
Mixed reviews for silicone makeup brush covers. Set comes 9 pieces, two sizes for foundation brushes and eyeshadow brushes, plus small ones with suction cups.

GOOD: Keeps brushes clean, protected, organized in bag. Great for travel. Prevents powder marks in bag. Colors cute. Large covers work well for right-sized brushes.

BAD: Sizing inconsistent. Large covers too tight for many brushes, risk damaging bristles. Small covers too tiny for most eyeshadow brushes. Suction cups weak, fall off after minute or two, even with water trick. Silicone grip so strong hard to slide on and off. Some covers ripped within days of use. Small hole meant for bristles bends and ruins them.

BOTTOM LINE: Works best for travel, not daily use. Large covers decent if brush fits. Small suction cup ones nearly useless. Buy only if brushes match sizing and expect struggle getting covers on and off.
